name: Potato Crepes with Scallops, Salmon and Asparagus
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the asparagus into 1 1/2-inch lengths using a diagonal rolling cut: Slice off the tip at an angle, roll the asparagus about a quarter turn and cut again, repeating down the stalk until you reach the tougher base. Discard the bases.
2. Dissolve the cornstarch in the white wine in a medium bowl. Add the egg white, white pepper, and mix lightly.
3. Remove the tough white muscle from each scallop and cut into 1/4-inch-thick discs.
4. Remove any pin bones from the salmon and slice across the width of the piece (parallel to the backbone) into 1/4-inch-thick rectangles.
5. Add the salmon, scallops to the egg mixture and gently toss to coat.
6. Preheat the oven to 175°F and set two dinner plates in it to warm.
7. Prepare Potato Crepes and place one on each plate. Keep warm in the oven.
8. In a clean, dry, heatproof container (such as a saucepan) with a heat-proof sieve on top, drain the scallops and salmon and discard marinade.
9. Heat a wok or deep skillet over high heat until it begins to smoke. Add oil. Wait 30 seconds before adding seafood (the oil will not be very hot yet; this is intentional).
10. Cook stirring gently to separate pieces, until salmon has lost its raw color. Pour contents of the wok into sieve.
11. Return the wok to the heat and stir-fry ginger and green onion in the clinging oil until fragrant.
12. Add pepper and asparagus and cook until it begins to brown.
13. Add chicken stock, soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar. Cover, and cook until asparagus is crisp-tender (2-3 minutes depending on size).
14. Remove cover, return seafood to wok and stir in cornstarch mixture. Cook until sauce slightly thickens.
15. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Divide seafood, asparagus, and sauce over potato crepes. Serve immediately. Serves 2.
